definition:
νοιεω νοέω, -ῶ 
 (νοῦς), [in LXX chiefly for בִּין, also for שָׂכַל hi., etc ;] 
  1. to perceive with the mind, understand (for the phrase νοῶν κ. φρονῶν, in wills, see MM, xvii): absol., Mat.16:9, Mrk.8:17; with accusative, Eph.3:4, 1Ti.1:7; with dative instr., τ. καρδίᾳ, Jhn.12:40; pass., Rom.1:20; before ὅτι, Mat.15:17 16:11, Mrk.7:18; with accusative and inf., Heb.11:8. 
   2. to think, consider: absol., Mat.24:15, Mrk.13:14, Eph.3:20; with accusative of thing(s), 2Ti.2:7 
 (cf. -εὐ, κατα-, μετα-, προ-, ὑπο-νοέω).†
 (AS)
